text.title: Exploratory Testing Charters Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Exploratory Testing Charters application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Charter = explicit mission: 'Explore <area> with <tools/heuristics> to discover <quality-attribute issues>'. Vague missions = vague results.
rule.rationale: Bach/Bolton ET literature: charters are the unit of value; without them, ET drifts into random clicking.

rule.statement: Sessions are 60-120 minutes (90 default). Longer = fatigue; shorter = insufficient depth.
rule.rationale: Cognitive-load research: focused exploration peaks at 60-90 min; sessions ≥2h have diminishing returns.

rule.statement: Maintain a heuristics catalogue (SFDIPOT, CRUSSPIC STMPL, FEW HICCUPPS). Each session selects 2-4 heuristics; rotating across sessions covers the space.
rule.rationale: Without heuristics, ET is intuition; with them, it's systematic + transferable to new testers.

rule.statement: Every session ends with a written debrief: 1-2 paragraphs of what was tested, bugs found, follow-up areas. Stored in shared space.
rule.rationale: Tacit knowledge from sessions vanishes within 48h; debrief converts to org capability.

rule.statement: ≥30% of sessions are pair-tested OR PMW (Person, Mission, Witness). Solo ET still works but pair sessions find 30-50% more bugs.
rule.rationale: Empirical studies (Bach, Lyndsay) show pair ET finds substantially more defects than solo.
